LOOK.text: Three apes in the shallow end, scrolling phones in waterproof cases. Their screens all show the same chart. None of them are looking at each other.

TALK.text: 'Bro, we're providing liquidity.' 'To what?' 'To this pool. We're literally IN the liquidity pool.' The other apes nod. One adds: 'I sold ETH at $400.' Solemnly, they all pour one out. Into the pool. Where they're standing.

USE.text: I try to engage them. One looks up briefly. 'Are you bullish or bearish?' he asks, then goes back to his phone before I can answer.

PICK.text: They're in a pool. I'm not fishing out crypto influencers. That's how you get rugged.

OPEN.text: One of them holds up his phone. 'Check my portfolio.' I look. It's a picture of a sunset. 'That's not a portfolio.' 'It is now.'

CLOSE.text: I try to end the conversation. 'NFA!' one yells as I walk away. They all say it. In unison. Like a support group mantra.

LOOK.text: More apes, deeper in the pool. One wears a diamond floatie. Another has a pink donut ring. They all look like they're 'making it' while literally going under.

TALK.text: 'Welcome to the liquidity pool! We ARE the liquidity.' I look around. Apes floating in green water. 'So THIS is what happens to liquidity providers.' 'We don't leave. Ever. That's the impermanent part.'

USE.text: I try to hand one of them a towel. 'No thanks, I'm HODLing... the water.' He sinks a little lower.

PICK.text: They're slippery. In more ways than one.

OPEN.text: 'Open your mind,' one says. 'I'll open my wallet when you show me a real roadmap,' I reply. Silence. Then: 'Fair.'

CLOSE.text: 'Close your positions!' I yell. They all laugh nervously. One starts crying. That escalated quickly.

then.text: A giant rubber duck floatie in the pool. My rubber duck vibrates in my pocket. They can sense each other. This is either cute or terrifying.

else.text: A massive rubber duck floatie. The apes treat it with reverence. 'That's Gerald,' one whispers. 'He's been here since the ICO era.'

TALK.text: I address the duck directly. 'Gerald, what's the alpha?' The duck says nothing. The apes gasp. 'He only speaks during bull markets,' one explains.

then.text: I place my rubber duck next to Gerald. They float side by side. The apes go silent. 'The prophecy...' one whispers. 'Two ducks... the merge is complete.'

else.text: I try to ride Gerald. An ape blocks me. 'That's a sacred artifact. You need at least a rubber duck offering to approach.' A rubber duck? Where would I find one of those?

PICK.text: Gerald is too big to pick up. And the apes would riot. Never come between a degen and their inflatable.

OPEN.text: There's a valve on Gerald's belly. I could deflate him, but that feels like a war crime in this context.

CLOSE.text: Gerald's beak is sealed. Like most crypto NDAs.

LOOK.text: A TV mounted on the wall showing live crypto charts. Green candles. The apes in the pool cheer. Red candle. They go underwater. It's a synchronized swimming routine driven by market sentiment.

TALK.text: 'Change the channel!' I yell. 'There IS no other channel,' the bartender robot replies. 'Trust me, I've tried.'

USE.text: I try to change the channel. The remote is at the bottom of the pool. Nobody dares dive for it. 'Last guy who tried found a liquidation notice down there,' an ape warns.

PICK.text: It's bolted to the wall. Like my eyes to these charts. We're all trapped.

OPEN.text: I open the TV settings. The only option is 'MORE CHARTS'. I close it.

CLOSE.text: I try to turn it off. 'NO!' scream all the apes in unison. I step back slowly.

LOOK.text: A well-stocked bar with bottles of various colors. The labels read: 'Liquid Staking', 'Proof of Drink', 'Gas Fee Gin', 'Rug Pull Rum'. A robot bartender polishes a glass that's already spotless.

TALK.text: I approach the bar. The robot looks up. 'What'll it be? We have everything except real financial returns.'

USE.text: I sit at the bar. The robot slides me a glass of green liquid. 'On the house. It's our signature drink: The Liquidation.' It tastes like regret and lime.

PICK.text: I grab a bottle labeled 'Yield Farming Whiskey'. The robot snatches it back. 'That's aged 3 epochs. Not for amateurs.'

OPEN.text: I open a bottle. The fizz sounds like a transaction being confirmed. 'That's the impermanent loss IPA,' the robot explains. 'The taste changes depending on when you drink it.'

CLOSE.text: I close the bottle cap. 'Smart,' the robot says. 'Most people here can't close a position either.'

LOOK.text: A chrome robot with LED eyes and a bowtie. It moves with mechanical precision, mixing drinks with algorithmic accuracy. A nametag reads: 'B.O.T. — Beverage Operations Terminal'. Below it, scratched in: 'formerly: Trading Bot v2.3'.

USE.text: I try to access the robot's control panel. 'Buy me a drink first,' it says. Even robots have boundaries.

PICK.text: It weighs 200kg and has a taser arm. I'll pass.

OPEN.text: I open the robot's chest panel. Inside: a tiny hamster wheel. 'That's my backup generator,' it says, closing the panel. 'Don't judge.'

CLOSE.text: 'Please don't try to shut me down. Last time someone did that, I auto-bought 10,000 DOGE.'

LOOK.text: Banana peels scattered around the pool edge. The apes are messy guests. Or it's performance art. Hard to tell with this crowd.

TALK.text: I talk to a banana peel. This is rock bottom. Or is it the pool bottom? Both, really.

USE.text: I slip on a banana peel. Classic. The apes rate my fall: '7/10, nice form, weak landing.'

PICK.text: I pick up a banana peel. An ape looks offended. 'That's... that's organic decentralized compost.' I put it back.

OPEN.text: You can't open a banana peel. You can unpeel a banana, but that ship has sailed. Or slipped.

CLOSE.text: I try to reassemble the banana. The apes watch in fascination. 'Is that... reverse yield farming?'

LOOK.text: A pile of discarded items near the stairs: empty energy drink cans, torn whitepapers, a broken phone with 'WAGMI' as the wallpaper. Looks like the apes have been using this corner as a landfill. There might be something worth salvaging in there.

TALK.text: I address the pile. 'What treasures do you hold?' A can falls off the top. That's either a sign or gravity.

USE.text: I rummage through the junk. Mostly worthless. Like an altcoin portfolio in January.

then.text: Nothing else worth salvaging. Just broken dreams and banana peels.

else.text: I dig through the pile and push aside some crumpled whitepapers and an alarming number of banana peels. Wait. There's a rubber duck in here? How did this survive the carnage?
It squeaks when I pick it up. The apes look up from their phones. 'Is that... a duck?' one whispers reverently. 'Gerald will want to meet it.'

OPEN.text: I open a crumpled paper from the pile. It reads: 'TO DO: 1. Buy the dip. 2. Buy the dip again. 3. Realize there is no bottom.' Motivational.

CLOSE.text: I push the trash back together. An ape nods approvingly. 'Composability,' he says. I don't think he knows what that word means.

LOOK.text: A heavy metal door, pitch black behind the gap. A sign reads: 'DEEP LIQUIDITY — AUTHORIZED WHALES ONLY'. Below it, someone scratched: 'abandon all stablecoins, ye who enter here.'

USE.text: I reach for the handle. A cold draft pushes back. From inside, I hear the sound of margin calls echoing in the void. Nope. Not today. Not ever.

OPEN.text: I try the door. It opens an inch. The darkness inside is so complete it feels like staring into an unlisted token's order book. I close it immediately.

TALK.text: I knock on the door. Something knocks back. Three times. Then silence. Then a faint whisper: 'What's your leverage?' I back away slowly.

PICK.text: I can't pick up a door that leads to the abyss. The abyss picks YOU.

CLOSE.text: It's already closed. Good. Whatever's behind there has more TVL than sense.

PUSH.text: I push the door. It groans. An ape behind me grabs my shoulder. 'Don't. That's the deep end. Nobody comes back from the deep end with their portfolio intact.'

PULL.text: I pull the door. Cold air hits my face. Smells like leveraged positions and regret. A screen glows inside showing only one word: 'REKT'. I let go.

LOOK.text: Stairs leading back up to the clinic reception. The sound of splashing and impermanent loss fades behind me.

TALK.text: The stairs creak as if they're disappointed in my life choices. Join the club, stairs.

PICK.text: I can't pick up stairs. That's not a thing. Not even in Web3.

CLOSE.text: There's no door to close. The apes' poor financial decisions echo freely up the stairwell.

LOOK.text: 'The Art of the Rug: 48 Laws of DeFi Power'. Author: Anonymous. The foreword is by 'a whale who wishes to remain unnamed'. The back cover QR code leads to a rickroll.

TALK.text: 'Has anyone read this?' An ape scoffs. 'Read it? I LIVED it. Chapter 7: How to Lose Friends and Liquidate People. That's my autobiography.'

USE.text: I flip to a random page. 'Rule #23: If someone says trust me bro, run. If they say NFA, run faster.' Sound advice from a book found next to a pool of degens.

PICK.text: I pick it up. Heavier than expected. The pages are stiff with dried pool water and what I hope is just highlighter ink. I put it back.

OPEN.text: Chapter 1: 'Buy High, Sell Low — A Beginner's Guide'. Chapter 2: 'I Did My Own Research (On Twitter)'. Chapter 3: 'Why Your Favorite Influencer Doesn't Own What They Shill'. I slam it shut.

CLOSE.text: A bookmark falls out. It's a receipt for 3 ETH worth of $SAFU. Current value: $0.00. The bookmark IS the lesson.

LOOK.text: A water-damaged blue notebook. Cover reads: 'MY TRADING JOURNAL — Day 1 to Lambo'. Someone crossed out 'Lambo' and wrote 'Ramen'. Then crossed out 'Ramen' and wrote 'Foodbank'. Then circled back to 'Lambo' with a note: 'copium'.

TALK.text: 'Whose journal is this?' An ape raises his hand. 'Day 47: Portfolio down 80%. Mood: bullish. Day 48: Down 95%. Mood: this is fine. Day 49: Up 2%. Mood: I'M A GENIUS.'

USE.text: I open to today's entry. It just says 'wen moon' repeated 47 times. The handwriting gets progressively more unhinged. The last one is in crayon.

PICK.text: Pages fall out like autumn leaves. Each one has a different cope: 'zoom out', 'still early', 'diamond hands', 'generational wealth'. It's a scrapbook of denial.

OPEN.text: Back cover pocket has a folded chart. Every dip annotated: 'bought here', 'bought here too', 'and here', 'why did I buy here', 'I need help', 'THIS IS THE BOTTOM' (it wasn't).

CLOSE.text: The last page: 'If you're reading this, don't feel sorry for me. I'm in a pool now. Providing liquidity. Living my best life.' A single tear stains the ink.

greeting.speaker: B.O.T.
greeting.text: Welcome to the Liquidity Pool. I'm B.O.T. — Beverage Operations Terminal. Formerly a high-frequency trading algorithm. Now I mix drinks and watch apes provide liquidity. With their bodies. It's... a lateral move.

what_is_this.speaker: B.O.T.
what_is_this.text: Dr. Satoshi's experimental DeFi therapy. Literal liquidity provision. The apes believe being IN the pool makes them liquidity providers. The green water is infused with 'decentralized minerals'. It's just food coloring. But the APY on their delusions is remarkable.

therapy_works.speaker: B.O.T.
therapy_works.text: Define 'work'. They haven't checked their portfolios in 45 minutes. That's a clinic record. One of them even made eye contact with another human yesterday. Baby steps.

patients.speaker: B.O.T.
patients.text: Chronic cases, all of them. That one bought the top of every cycle since 2017. That one over there? Mortgaged his house for a dog coin. And the one with the diamond floatie? He's actually profitable. He just comes for the vibes.

profitable.speaker: B.O.T.
profitable.text: Survivor's guilt. He made money while his friends got liquidated. Now he floats here, pretending to be broke. It's the crypto version of a support group. 'Hi, I'm Chad, and I sold the top.' *gasps from the pool*

patient_zero.speaker: B.O.T.
patient_zero.text: Patient Zero... *servos whir* My data logs show someone matching that description. They were here before the pool was a pool. When this was still a server room. They left something behind — a message in my memory banks I can't delete.

message.speaker: B.O.T.
message.text: 'The cure isn't in the code. It's in the community.' That's all. Encrypted with a key I don't have. Whoever Patient Zero was, they understood something the rest of us are still trying to compute.

when.speaker: B.O.T.
when.text: Block 1. Not block 1 of Base. Block 1 of this building. Before AdrianLAB was AdrianLAB. Before the trading floors and the diamond protocol. When it was just... someone's dream in a basement. Like all the best things.

drink.speaker: B.O.T.
drink.text: The house special: 'The Liquidation'. It's green, it burns, and you'll question your life choices afterward. Just like real trading. Cheers.

farewell.speaker: B.O.T.
farewell.text: Come back anytime. The pool is always open. The bar is always stocked. And the charts... the charts never sleep. Neither do I. Because I'm a robot. That was the joke. I'll work on my delivery.
